---
title: "DiagonalMetric"
generated: true
description: "Per-feature axis-aligned metric $M =  operatorname{diag}(1 /  sigma^2)$."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.smoothing.metric`](../nitrix-smoothing-metric.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:DiagonalMetric -->
```python
class DiagonalMetric
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:DiagonalMetric -->
Per-feature axis-aligned metric $M = \operatorname{diag}(1 / \sigma^2)$.

The classic bilateral metric: each feature channel $d$ is
divided by its own bandwidth $\sigma_d$ before the squared
distance is accumulated.  A large $\sigma_d$ means channel
$d$ contributes little to the weight (the filter is near-blind
to that feature); in the limit $\sigma_d \to \infty$ the
channel drops out entirely.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:DiagonalMetric.project -->
```python
project(deltas: Float[Array, '... d_f']) -> Float[Array, '... d_f']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Divide each feature difference by its per-channel bandwidth.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:DiagonalMetric.tree_flatten -->
```python
tree_flatten() -> Tuple[Tuple[Float[Array, 'd_f']], None]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:DiagonalMetric.tree_unflatten -->
```python
tree_unflatten(_aux: None, children: Tuple[Any, ...]) -> 'DiagonalMetric'
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:DiagonalMetric.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(sigma: Float[Array, 'd_f']) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:DiagonalMetric -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `d_f` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
